extraction.one_line_summary: EVENEPOEL Remco won stage 7 in a two-up sprint with ROGLIC after Quick-Step's relentless chase shredded all domestiques, leaving GC rivals to attack/chase themselves in final 26km.
tactical_patterns: Soudal Quick-Step's sustained pace on circuits eliminated all domestiques before decisive moves, forcing leaders into solo tactical decisions.
UAE's team cohesion failed: SOLER marked moves while AYATES helped chase, fragmenting their ability to contest ROGLIC/EVENEPOEL dominance.
EVENEPOEL attacked solo at 26km when Quick-Step domestiques exhausted, demonstrating willingness to race offensively despite time gap pressure.
Circuit races with 8km laps and technical descents favor duo breakaways over solo attacks when GC contenders are evenly matched.

rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: positive: Won two stages and mountains jersey while finishing 6 seconds down on GC, showing form progression through week
rider_signals.ROGLIC Primoz: positive: Controlled race through team pacing then marked EVENEPOEL personally in finale, referenced as 'unbeatable' after Tirreno win
rider_signals.SOLER Marc: neutral: Rode for own placement finishing third while teammate AYATES chased, exposing UAE's unclear tactical hierarchy
rider_signals.AYATES Adam: negative: Helped chase down teammate SOLER's group, demonstrating team confusion in critical GC moments
